title: Saudi Vice Minister of Defense Holds Talks with U.S Officials
content: His Royal Highness Prince Khalid bin Salman Al Saud, Vice Minister of Defense of Saudi Arabia visited Washington on Monday and held talks with senior U.S. administration officials. The Vice Minister met with President Donald J. Trump at the White House and discussed the importance of strong bilateral relations between the two nations. HRH Prince Khalid also met with Secretary of State Michael Pompeo, Secretary of Defense Mark Esper and National Security Advisor Robert O’Brien.The Vice Minister discussed the latest developments in the Middle East with his American counterparts and the joint efforts to reduce tensions and avoid escalations that could further destabilize the region in light of the Iranian regime’s provocations and destabilizing activities. The Vice Minister also expressed his appreciation for the 75-year partnership between Saudi Arabia and the United States, and reiterated the Kingdom’s commitment to working closely with the United States to reduce instability in the region and address the two countries’ shared security concerns.

title: Saudi Arabia in Focus
content:
Saudi Arabia JUNE 2017 
Government
Inside the Issue
King Salman Names New Crown Prince, Interior Minister
Foreign Minister: Demands That Qatar Stop Funding Terrorism Are Non-Negotiable 
Foreign Minster Meets With U.S.Secretary of State  
White Paper: Saudi Arabia and the Visit of President Trump   
Saudi-Owned TV Channel Fights Daesh Propaganda With New Drama Series  
Crown Prince Directs $66.7M to Fight Cholera in Yemen Saudi Arabia Achieves Majority of UN Development Goals Companies Collaborate to Release Report on Saudi Women in Business
Government: King Salman Names New Crown Prince, Interior Minister 
On June 21, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued a number of royal orders, including the appointment of Prince Mohammed bin Salman as Crown Prince and Deputy Prime Minister.
The Crown Prince will continue in his role as Minister of Defense and other missions previously assigned to him.
Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Naif bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud was named Minister of Interior, and Dr.Ahmed bin Mohammed Al-Salem was appointed Deputy Minister of Interior at the rank of Minister.
President Trump called Prince Mohammed to congratulate him on his new position.
During the call, the President and the Crown Prince reiterated the commitment of the U.S.
and Saudi Arabia to close cooperation to advance stability and security in the Middle East and across the world.
Regional Security: 
Foreign Minister: Demands That Qatar Stop Funding Terrorism Are Non-Negotiable 
On June 27, Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met with a group of journalists in Washington to brief them on the ongoing diplomatic situation with Qatar.
Saudi Arabia – along with the USaudinited Arab Emirates, Bahrain and Egypt – suspended diplomatic ties with Qatar on June 5 as a result of its ongoing financial support for terrorist and extremist organizations.
Arabia  Minister Al-Jubeir emphasized that Saudi Arabia’s demands are non-negotiable.
The decision to take diplomatic action against Qatar was made after consideration of Doha’s long history of behavior that includes harboring known terrorists and funding extremist groups throughout the region.
“This idea that you can fund extremist groups, that you can pay ransom to terrorist groups like Al-Qaeda and ISIS, that you can send $300 million to the Shi’ite militias in Iraq with most of it ending up with the Quds Force in Iran, is not acceptable,” Minister Al-Jubeir said.
“I think most countries in the world would agree with the demand to stop this.” 
“We have to have policy of zero tolerance,” he asserted.
“And unless we have that, we’re not going to be able to be effective in fighting terrorism or terror financing or extremism.”
BIL ATERAL REL ATIONS Foreign Minster Meets With U.S.Secretary of State Foreign Minister 
Al-Jubeir met with U.S.Secretary of State Rex Tillerson at the State Department on June 14, where the two parties discussed joint efforts to combat terrorism and extremism, along with other issues pertinent to regional and international cooperation.
Before the meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir told reporters that there is no blockade of Qatar.
“Qatar is free to go – the ports are open, the airports are open.
What we have done is we have denied them use of our airspace, and this is our sovereign right.
The limitation on the use of Saudi airspace is only limited to Qatari airways or Qatari-owned aircraft, not anybody else.
The seaports of Qatar are open; there is no blockade on them.
Qatar can move goods in and out whenever they want; they just cannot use our territorial waters,” he said.
“We have exercised our sovereign right to prohibit them from using our airspace.
We have allowed the movement of families between the two countries to happen so that we don’t divide families, and we are prepared to provide Qatar with food and medical supplies, should they need this, through the King Salman Center.” 
While in Washington, Minister Al-Jubeir also met with members of Congress.
White Paper: Saudi Arabia and the Visit of President TrumpThe Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a white paper titled “Saudi Arabia and the Visit of President Trump,” providing an in-depth look at the major events and accomplishments that occurred during the U.S.President’s first visit to Saudi Arabia in May 2017.
The historic visit of President Trump to Saudi Arabia bolstered the longstanding Saudi-U.S.
relationship and expanded economic cooperation.
Fifty-five leaders and representatives from across the Arab and Islamic world gathered in the Kingdom during the two-day visit, for dialogue and exchange, uniting under one shared vision – together we prevail.
The new report, available on the embassy website, outlines the achievements of the three summits held and the major business opportunities that resulted from the first-ever Saudi-U.S.
CEO Summit, held the same weekend.
COUNTERTERRORISM
Saudi Arabia Saudi-Owned TV Channel Fights Daesh Propaganda With New Drama SeriesMBC, a private Saudi-owned television station, has launched a new drama series that aims to fight Daesh (ISIS) propaganda by depicting the violence and brutality faced by those who join the organization.
to radicalize and attract vulnerable people, pushing a grossly false view of life in the terror organization.
“Media is part of their (Daesh) offensive strategy.
Thus media organizations have the right, actually the duty, to face such an offensive – which is well-funded and on the internet and social media – with this series,” MBC spokesman Mazen Hayek told Reuters, which featured the new drama this month.
“Black Crows,” shown across the Arab world, demonstrates the cruelty to which Daesh recruits are subjected.
The show is meant to counter the propaganda promoted by Daesh, which uses the media 
HUMANITARIAN AID
Crown Prince Directs $66.7M to Fight Cholera in Yemen
Saudi Arabia announced a donation of $66.7 million to the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF), the World Health Organization (WHO) and their partners to respond to the outbreak of cholera in Yemen.
The King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSrelief) will work with the organizations to implement the new initiative immediately.
“We will continue to work with our partners across a broad range of humanitarian and relief efforts for the people of Yemen,” he said.
Dr.Mahmoud Fikri, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, commented on the importance of the donation.
“The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is committed to working closely with our aid partners to effectively address the cholera and general humanitarian situation in Yemen.
We have listened to the call from UNICEF and WHO for an immediate donation of $66.ss7 million to address cholera specifically, and have acted accordingly,” said Dr.Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Al-Rabeeah, Adviser at the Royal Court and Supervisor of KSrelief.
“On behalf of the WHO Regional Office I extend deep thanks and appreciation to His Royal Highness, Prince Mohammad bin Salman, Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence for his noble initiative in the humanitarian field, to support efforts which aim to alleviate suffering, provide relief through working to contain cholera and prevent its complications in Yemen,” he said.
Development
Saudi Arabia Achieves Majority of UN Development Goals 
Saudi Arabia has surpassed a majority of the 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by adopting structural reforms in their government.
and Planning, announced the achievement in a speech at the Second Forum of Economic and Social Council on Financing for Development, held at UN headquarters.
Abdullah Al-Marwani, assistant undersecretary for Planning and National Policies at the Ministry of Economy
Saudi Arabia“The Kingdom has already achieved most of the overall goals of sustainable development of the 17 goals of the UN,” Al-Marwani said.
“Sometimes these achievements exceeded the goals of the UN Agenda 2030 with the  average annual economic growth of the Kingdom amounting to about 3.6 percent, higher than the global average.” Al-Marwani pointed out that Vision 2030, and the associated National Transformation Program (NTP), have pushed Saudi Arabia toward achievement of the SDGs by increasing investment initiatives in human capital, infrastructure, education, health and alternative energy.
The ultimate goal of Vision 2030 and the NTP is to diversify and transform the Saudi economy and to improve the lives of Saudi citizens.
Other financial and health goals laid out in the UN SDGs have also been met.
“In addition, the overall financing is progressing at a steady pace where the domestic credit ratio provided by the financial sector increased from 1.8 percent in 2010 to 20.8 percent in 2015.
One of the most important goals achieved in the health sector in the Kingdom is life expectancy at birth, which has increased from 72.5 in 2000 to 74.5 in 2015,” he said.
Companies Collaborate to Release Report on Saudi Women in BusinessMore Saudi women are in the job market than ever before, according to a new report released as a collaborative effort between two companies analyzing the role of women in business and leadership in Saudi Arabia.
Glowork, a company founded by young Saudi entrepreneurs aiming to empower women and increase diversity in the Saudi workplace, partnered with AccountAbility – a leading global research, consulting and standards organization – to assess the landscape of women in management and leadership in the Kingdom.
The 40-page report titled “The Women in Management and Leadership in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia 2017” analyzed data from a survey of more than 300 companies of varying sizes in the Kingdom.
“What this shows is that there are more women in the job market place than ever before,” Glowork founder and CEO Khalid Al Khudair told Arabia Now.
“There are also more women in senior positions too, so now we can see more benchmarks and it’s shown that they’ve got to where they need to be.
Women have always been in the job market in Saudi Arabia, but now they are increasing their presence in other sectors too, so things are really progressing well.”One of the key findings in the report is that three in four survey participants acknowledged that engaging female employments leads to productivity gains.
Additionally, it was found that organizations in Saudi Arabia are increasingly providing professional development structures for their female employees.
The report emphasizes that Saudi Arabia is in a period of transformation in terms of women in the workforce.
“The future is looking brighter,” said Al Khudair.

title: Saudi Arabia in Focus
content:
Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz welcomed President Donald J. Trump to Riyadh, Saudi Arabia on May 20, 2017. This was the first time a U.S. president traveled to Saudi Arabia on his first foreign trip. The two countries engaged in a series of talks aimed at reaffirming and strengthening the longstanding Saudi-U.S. partnership. These talks resulted in the formulation of the Joint Strategic Vision Declaration, which ensured that both nations will dedicate themselves to strengthening their Strategic Partnership for the 21st Century.  Saudi Arabia
MAY 2017
Saudi Arabia Welcomes President Trump 
INSIDE THIS ISSUE 
President Trump Makes Historic Visit to Saudi ArabiaSaudi Arabia Unveils Center for Combating Extremism Three Summits, One Vision: President Trump Ramps Up Regional Engagement in Riyadh  
Saudi Arabia and U.S.to Co-Chair Terrorist Financing Targeting Center   
Saudi-U.S.CEO Summit Yields Billions in Investments
Arms Deal Bolsters Saudi Defense, Creates U.S.Jobs
President Trump Makes Historic Visit to Saudi Arabia 
In May, United States President Donald J.Trump made history: For the first time, a U.S.President traveled to Saudi Arabia on his first foreign trip, engaging in discussions with Saudi Arabia and other nations that resulted in several agreements and, ultimately, the strengthening of the critical Saudi-U.S.relationship.
The two countries engaged in a series of talks aimed at reaffirming the longstanding Saudi-U.S.partnership and strengthening the close ties between the two countries.
These talks resulted in the formulation of the Joint Strategic Vision Declaration, which ensured that both nations will dedicate themselves to strengthening their Strategic Partnership for the 21st Century.
According to the declaration, “The United States of America and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia seek to embark on new initiatives to counter violent extremist messaging, disrupt financing of terrorism, and advance defense cooperation.”
“As we extend our thanks to and appreciation of [President Trump] for his response to attend and participate in this summit, we reaffirm our happiness and gratitude for His Excellency’s choice of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and this summit as His Excellency’s first trip and external participation since assuming office.” 
“This historic and unprecedented gathering of leaders—unique in the history of nations—is a symbol to the world of our shared resolve and our mutual respect.– Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, May 21, 2017 
To the leaders and citizens of every country assembled here today, I want you to know that the United States is eager to form closer bonds of friendship, security, culture and commerce.” – President Donald J. Trump, May 21, 2017
Furthermore, the declaration lays out a vision for a “robust, integrated regional security architecture,” recognizing that such infrastructure is necessary for successful cooperation.
The two countries will form a Strategic Joint Consultative Group to chart the course of the strategic partnership.
The group will meet at least once per year, alternating between the two countries, to review areas of cooperation and continually strengthen efforts toward collaboration.
The two leaders also agreed on the necessity of containing Iran’s interference in the internal affairs of Saudi Arabia and other regional states, encouragement of sectarianism, continued support of terrorism and militant proxy groups, and efforts to destabilize the region.
Additionally, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and President Trump recognized the importance of economic ties between Saudi Arabia and the U.S., and took steps to increase trade and investment, develop joint economic projects, and create jobs in both countries.
The visit resulted in investment and trade agreements with a potential value of more than $350 billion.
Saudi Arabia Unveils Center for Combating Extremism
On May 21, King Salman inaugurated the new Global Center for Combating Extremist Ideology.
President Donald Trump joined the King for the opening of the Center, further underlining the close cooperation that Saudi Arabia and the U.S.enjoy in combating extremism.
The Center, headquartered in Riyadh, builds upon and extends the growing international cooperation in combating extremist ideology, and will use innovative techniques to fight the growth of terrorism and destroy its allure for vulnerable young people by promoting moderation and encouraging positive dialogue.
The Center will monitor, process and analyze extremist speech online in a number of languages and dialects, relying on technology that can process and analyze data in six seconds.
King Salman and President Trump sign investment agreements, May 20, 2017.
Three Summits, One Vision: President Trump Ramps Up Regional Engagement in Riyadh
Following the unprecedented Saudi-U.S.Summit, President Donald Trump participated in two other major forums during his visit to Riyadh: a GCC-U.S.Summit and the Arab Islamic American Summit.
All participants are hopeful that the administration’s commitment to ramp up engagement and dialogue will help solve the region’s most pressing problems.
The GCC-U.S.summit yielded the reaffirmation of the strategic partnership between the two parties.
A number of issues were discussed, including a commitment to tackling the root causes of crises in the Middle East, the need for more urgent steps to address threats posed by Daesh (ISIS) and Al-Qaeda, and the rejection of Iran’s continued interference in the internal affairs of the GCC states and other regional countries.
King Salman expressed his optimism about the outcomes of the meetings in his remarks at the Arab Islamic American Summit on May 21.
“The meeting significantly shows that our 55 Arab and Islamic countries attending the summit today with a population exceeding 1.5 billion, are an important partner in fighting the forces of extremism and terrorism and achieving world peace, security and stability,” said King Salman.
“His Excellency [President Trump] holds many hopes and aspirations for cooperation with the Arab and Muslim world.” President Trump also gave a speech in which he spelled out the necessity for cooperation between the U.S.and the entire region.
“You also hosted me in the treasured home of King Abdulaziz, the founder of the Kingdom who united your great people,” President Trump remarked.
“Working alongside another beloved leader – American President Franklin Roosevelt – King Abdulaziz began the enduring partnership between our two countries.
King Salman: your father would be so proud to see that you are continuing his legacy – and just as he opened the first chapter in our partnership, today we begin a new chapter that will bring lasting benefits to our citizens.” President Trump laid out the U.S.commitment to work alongside Arab and Muslim nations to defeat terrorism and extremism.
“This is not a battle between different faiths, different sects, or different civilizations,” he said.
“This is a battle between barbaric criminals who seek to obliterate human life, and decent people of all religions who seek to protect it.
This is a battle between good and evil.” This sentiment was echoed by Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir at a joint press conference with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.
“Unless we are able to move [away] from notions of a clash of civilizations and move towards a partnership among civilizations, we will not be able to eradicate the scourge of terrorism,” said Minister Al-Jubeir.
Saudi Arabia and U.S.to Co-Chair Terrorist Financing Targeting Center 
Saudi Arabia and the United States signed an agreement on May 21 announcing the intent to establish the Terrorist Financing Center (TFTC).
The TFTC marks a collaborative approach to confronting new and evolving threats from terrorist financing, leveraging existing tools and formalizing cooperation between the U.S., Saudi Arabia and partners in the Gulf.
U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven T. Mnuchin praised the agreement, pointing to the benefits of enhanced cooperation between the two countries.
“This new Terrorist Financing Targeting Center will enhance existing tools and cooperation with partners in the Gulf to forcefully address evolving threats,” Secretary Mnuchin said.
“Treasury will offer the vast expertise of our Office of Terrorism and Financial Intelligence to this creative new effort.
We will co-chair the TFTC with Saudi Arabia, and will work collaboratively with the countries of the Gulf Cooperation Council to counter these global terror networks.”
Saudi-U.S.CEO Summit Yields Billions in Investments
As President Donald Trump traveled to Saudi Arabia, another high profile event was being held in tandem in Riyadh: the inaugural Saudi-U.S.CEO Forum, attended by more than 100 CEOs and leaders of the most influential companies in Saudi Arabia and the U.S.Saudi and U.S.companies signed investment and trade agreements worth $350 billion.
The agreements are expected to drive economic growth and create jobs in both the U.S.and Saudi Arabia.
agreements demonstrate the fast diversification that the Kingdom’s economy is currently undergoing.
“The sheer breadth and scale of these agreements epitomize the accelerating diversification of the Kingdom’s economy into high-growth sectors such as technology, industrial manufacturing and aerospace, creating and safeguarding 250,000 jobs both in Saudi Arabia and the U.S.,” he said.
Secretary of State Rex Tillerson commented on the agreements during a press briefing with Foreign Minister Adel Al-Jubeir.
“We expect that these investments over the next 10 years or so will provide hundreds of thousands of jobs in both the United States and Saudi Arabia,” said Secretary Tillerson.
“They will lead to a transfer of technology from the U.S.to Saudi Arabia, enhance our economy, and also enhance American investments in Saudi Arabia, which already are the largest investments of anyone.”The agreements are in line with Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030 plan for economic development and reform.
Dr.Majed Al-Kassabi, Minister for Commerce and Investment in Saudi Arabia, emphasized that the The agreements covered a number of sectors, including defense, energy, oil and gas, chemicals, infrastructure, technology and digital solutions, healthcare and pharmaceuticals.
Minister of Energy, Industry and Mineral Resources Khalid Al-Falih participates in the Ministerial Panel at the Saudi–U.S.CEO Forum, May 20, 2017.
Arms Deal Bolsters Saudi Defense, Creates U.S.Jobs
While in Riyadh, President Trump and King Salman announced a defense package potentially worth more than $110 billion, aimed at building the security capabilities of Saudi Arabia and contributing to job creation in the U.S.The package of defense equipment will contribute to the long-term security of Saudi Arabia, increasing capabilities to fight terrorism in the region and effectively contribute to regional security.
The sale will include air force modernization, with improvements to systems and training that will enable the Kingdom to continue its air campaign against Daesh targets in Syria.
The arms sale demonstrates the U.S.commitment to Saudi Arabia, while expanding opportunities for American companies in the region and, according to the Trump administration, potentially supporting tens of thousands of new jobs in the United States.
Approximately 1.2 million Americans work in the aerospace and defense sectors.

title: Saudi Arabia in Focus
content:
Saudi Arabia APRIL 2017
SAUDI-U.S. RELATIONS
INSIDE THIS ISSUE
King Salman Appoints New Ambassador to Washington
Secretary of State Speaks at U.S.-Saudi CEO Summit
Op-ed: Saudi Ambassador Praises Saudi-U.S. Partnership   
White Paper: Saudi Arabia and the Yemen Conflict   
Saudi Provides $8.2 Billion in Humanitarian Aid to Yemen
Deputy Crown Prince Discusses Vision 2030 TransformationSaudi Minister of Commerce Discusses Vision 2030 in Washington
White Paper: Saudi Arabia and Counterterrorism
Secretary of Defense Travels to Saudi Arabia, Reaffirms Bilateral Security Ties
King Salman Appoints New Ambassador to Washington 
NOTABLE QUOTE
 “I’m pleased [to be here today] to reaffirm the very strong partnership that exists between the United State and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
On  April  22,  Custodian  of  the  Two  Holy  Mosques  King  Sal-man  bin  Abdulaziz  appointed  Prince  Khalid  bin  Salman  bin Abdulaziz  as  the  Kingdom’s  new  ambassador  to  the  United States.
We have a long relationship with Saudi Arabia that extends over 80 years, and our support for a strong and steady partner on economic cooperation remains as firm as ever.”
After graduating from the King Faisal Air Academy in Riyadh, Prince Khalid received pilot training in the United States at Co-lumbus  Air  Force  Base  in  Mississippi.
He  went  on  to  receive training on advanced electronic warfare in France.
Prince Khalid flew F15-S combat missions against Daesh (ISIS) as part of the Global Coalition Against Daesh and over Yemen as  part  of  Operations  Decisive  Storm  and  Renewal  of  Hope while on active duty in the Royal Saudi Air Force.
– Secretary of State Rex Tillerson, April 19, Remarks at the U.S.-Saudi Arabia CEO Summit
Secretary of State Speaks at U.S.-Saudi CEO SummitOn April 19, Secretary of State Rex Tillerson offered remarks at the U.S.-Saudi Arabia CEO Summit hosted by the U.S. Chamber of Commerce in Washington, D.C.leadership a strong and steady partner on these issues as well as on economic cooperation,” Sec.Tillerson stated.
Speaking on U.S.-Saudi trade relations and Vision 2030 reforms pursued by the Kingdom, Tillerson praised  Saudi Arabia for its engaged and active role in the international community.
“As we work together with Saudi Arabia on some of the most vexing problems confronting Middle East security and stability, we are very encouraged to find in the Saudi 
Sec.Tillerson also pointed to the friendly business environment of Saudi Arabia, which he said will likely produce an increase in business collaboration.
“When U.S. companies invest in the Saudi economy, everyone wins.
The U.S. creates jobs at home, and businesses in the Gulf region get the best business partners that the global marketplace has to offer,”  he asserted.
Op-ed: Saudi Ambassador Praises Saudi-U.S. Partnership
Ambassador Abdullah Al-Saud praised the Saudi-U.S. partnership and President Trump’s decision to order missile strikes in Syria in an April column in the Wall Street Journal.
“The U.S. has been working closely with us for a long time to combat terror groups and to deter Iran and Hezbollah, often in ways the public doesn’t see.
Our support has been critical, but there are certain definitive actions that only the U.S. can take, certain voids that only the U.S. can fill,” wrote Amb.
Al-Saud.
The Ambassador outlined the value of decisive American action in the region.
“We value our alliance with the U.S., and we believe that American power – and the demonstrated willingness to use it judiciously – can change the dynamics in the Middle East for the better.
In the end, it is American power, reinforcing and complementing the work of America’s allies in the region, that will bring stability and lasting peace,” he concluded.
YEMEN
White Paper: Saudi Arabia and the Yemen ConflictThe Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a white paper titled “Saudi Arabia and the Yemen Conflict,” providing an in-depth look into the historical and geopolitical aspects of the conflict.
and discusses the major strategic interests at stake in the country.
It also gives an overview of Iran’s destabilizing influence, Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula’s presence, Houthi violations and Saudi humanitarian aid to the people of Yemen.
The paper outlines the major achievements of the Saudi-led Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen The paper is available on the Saudi Embassy’s website.2 Saudi ArabiaSaudi Provides $8.2 Billion in Humanitarian Aid to YemenSaudi Arabia pledged a $150 million donation to support the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center (KSRelief) in Yemen during the Geneva aid conference on April 25.
Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeah, supervisor general for KSRelief, explained that the new donation comes as part of the $8.2 billion committed by the Kingdom within its humanitarian and developmental assistance to Yemen since April 2015.
Established in May 2015, KSRelief has provided health, nutrition, and sanitation aid and relief to 17 million people impacted by the conflict in Yemen.
KSRelief, in partnership with a number of international humanitarian organizations, has established more than 100 health facilities, provided emergency food aid and supplied more than 350,000 gallons of fuel to medical facilities.
Saudi Arabia remains the single-largest donor of humanitarian assistance to Yemen.
VISION 2030
Deputy Crown Prince Discusses Vision 2030 Transformation
A piece by David Ignatius titled “A young prince is reimagining Saudi Arabia.
Can he make his vision come true” was published on April 20 in The Washington Post.
The article profiled Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, who outlined the progress that the Kingdom has made on the Vision 2030 plan, one year after its public launch.
Prince Mohammed highlighted that the budget deficit has been cut and non-oil revenue is projected to grow another 12 percent this year.
He also detailed plans to privatize Saudi Aramco, which is set to take place next year and is a major component of the push to diversify the economy away from reliance on energy.
Another priority, he said, is mining – further exploration by the industry is expected to tap into an estimated $1.5 trillion in potential mineral wealth.
“We want, as the Saudi people, to enjoy the coming days, and concentrate on developing our society and developing ourselves as individuals and families, while retaining our religion and customs.
We will not continue to be in the post-’79 era,” he said.
Saudi Minister of Commerce Discusses Vision 2030 in Washington
The Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS) recently welcomed H.E.Dr. Majed Al-Qasabi, Saudi Minister of Commerce and Investment, to review the progress of implementing Vision 2030 reforms since the national strategy’s release in  April 2016.
Highlighting the successes of Vision 2030, Al-Qasabi declared that the Kingdom is experiencing record transformation and openness to international exchange.
Additionally, Al-Qasabi commended Saudi women and youth for their significant contributions to economic development and national reform.
The success of Vision 2030, he indicated, relies on society’s commitment to embrace the kind of wide-ranging change that will produce greater governmental transparency and a widening of strategic economic and educational opportunities for Saudi citizens.
Announcing the upcoming award of 15 new trade licenses to American companies, Al-Qasabi emphasized that the Trump administration is seeking to engage the Kingdom in “win-win” deals for the U.S. and Saudi Arabia.
Al-Qasabi also underlined the importance of Secretary Tillerson’s recent remarks at the U.S. Chamber of Commerce, noting that international investments will rise dramatically as a result of the historic 85-year bilateral partnership.
COUNTERTERRORISM
White Paper: Saudi Arabia and Counterterrorism
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a white paper titled “Saudi Arabia and Counterterrorism,” outlining Saudi Arabia’s efforts to combat the men, money and the mindset that terrorist organizations create and rely on.
Saudi Arabia has undertaken comprehensive efforts to combat terrorism.
Kingdom’s close cooperation with the international community to fight Daesh (ISIS), Al-Qaeda and their affiliates.
It also gives an in-depth look into its initiatives to counter terror financing and undermine the mindset that leads vulnerable individuals to support terrorism.
Secretary of Defense Travels to Saudi Arabia, Reaffirms Bilateral Security Ties
Secretary of Defense James Mattis traveled to Saudi Arabia for discussions on strengthening the Saudi-U.S. security partnership.
Secretary Mattis told reporters that Saudi Arabia remains a “pillar of [the U.S.] security framework for the region and for American interests.”
King Salman met with Secretary Mattis to discuss ways in which the Saudi-U.S. strategic relationship could be strengthened and broadened.
“It’s very clear that Saudi Arabia is stepping up to its regional role…at a key time Secretary Mattis condemned the subversive role of Iran in the region, asserting that “Everywhere you look, if there’s trouble in the region, you find Iran.in terms of trying to restore stability in this key region in the world,” he added.
” On Yemen, the Secretary aligned with Saudi Arabia’s view that a political solution is the only viable outcome to the conflict to ensure that it ends as expediently as possible.

title: Saudi Arabia in Focus
content:
This issue highlights the Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman’s first visit to the United States under the Trump administration, an exclusive interview by Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir, Saudi Arabia’s participation in the recent Counter Daesh Coalition meeting in Washington, D.C. and more.Saudi Arabia
SAUDI-U.S. RELATIONS
MARCH 2017
INSIDE THIS ISSUE
U.S. Visit of Deputy Crown Prince Focuses on Security, Economics
Foreign Minister Expresses Confidence in Administration in Interview with Fox News
Energy Minister: Saudi-US Relations Are in Alignment   
Saudi Arabia Participates in Counter Daesh Coalition Meeting   
Saudi Arabia Supports America’s Tough Stance on Terrorism, Iran
Saudi U.N.Delegation Hosts Yemen Coalition ExpertsSaudi Arabia Leads Efforts to Support Yemen’s Health Sector
Saudi Women Are Redefining Their Roles
NOTABLE QUOTE
“I believe that no two countries are working as closely in fighting terrorism, and in particular ISIS, as the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the United States.This has been the case for a number of years.
We are intensifying our efforts.”– Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir,March 23, 2017
U.S. Visit of Deputy Crown Prince Focuses on Security, Economics 
In his first visit to the United States under the new administration, Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met with President Donald Trump, Vice President Mike Pence and Secretary of Defense Jim Mattis to discuss the longstanding and strategic Saudi-U.S. relationship.
“I would like to commend again the historical and strategic relations between our two countries witnessing further development in all fields,” stated Prince Moham-med in a cable to President Trump upon his departure on March 17.
Following the March 14 White House meeting, the White House reaffirmed the importance of the relationship, characterizing it as “strong, broad and enduring.”
SAUDI-U.S. RELATIONS
Foreign Minister Expresses Confidence in Administration in Interview with Fox News
On March 23, Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir held an exclusive interview with James Rosen of Fox News.
In response to questions about President Trump, Minister Al-Jubeir remarked, “I believe that he has vision.
I believe that he understands the importance of having America playing a big role in the world.We support that.”
Minister Al-Jubeir also commented on the state of security in the Middle East, emphasizing that Iran has intensified its covert actions and military activities in the Middle East following the implementation of the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA).
“I believe they [administration] will be less forgiving of Iran’s violations of the missile accords, its threats to maritime security in the Bab el-Mandab area, and in the Strait of Hormuz.
I believe they will be very strict in enforcing the nuclear agreement to the letter,” he said.
Energy Minister: Saudi-US Relations Are in Alignment
In an interview with Bloomberg’s Kathleen Hays on March 16, Energy Minister Khaled Al-Falih commented on the Saudi-US relationship following the meeting with Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed and President Trump.
Minister Al-Falih was part of the official delegation.
“The visit gives us a chance to refresh, renew and revive that relationship and take it to a higher level, which has been achieved by near-perfect alignment on geopolitical, security as well as economic points of views,” said Minister Al-Falih.
“That has been strengthened even further by the great rapport and bonding that was created between President Trump and HRH Prince Mohammed bin Salman as well as between the ministers and the delegations on both sides as we establish channels of communication, cooperation and driving the issues that are important for our two countries.”
COUNTERTERRORISM
Saudi Arabia Participates in Counter Daesh Coalition Meeting
Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir participated in a ministerial meeting of the Global Coalition Against Daesh (ISIS) in Washington on March 22, once again emphasizing Saudi Arabia’s commitment to standing with its international partners against terrorism across the world.
Saudi Arabia was one of the founding members of the coalition.
Minister Al-Jubeir held a separate meeting with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson the day after the meeting took place.
“I believe that no two countries are working as closely in fighting terrorism, and in particular ISIS, as the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the United States.
This has been the case for a number of years.
We are intensifying our efforts,” Minister Al-Jubeir told reporters.
The conference, he stated, “was huge success and reinforced the determination of the civilized world to take on this evil and to defeat it.” 
Saudi Arabia Supports America’s Tough Stance on Terrorism, Iran
In an op-ed on FoxNews.com on March 26, Adviser to the Saudi Defense Minister and Spokesman for the Saudi-led Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen Brigadier General Ahmed Asseri asserted Saudi Arabia’s commitment to supporting America’s tough stance against Daesh, terrorism and Iran.
“While the US-Saudi partnership is time-tested, reaffirming this relationship is a matter of strategy, not sentiment,” wrote Gen. Asseri.
Gen. Asseri emphasized that the Saudi-U.S. partnership is indispensable as the Kingdom confronts a host of challenges in the region, including putting an end to Iranian interference in Yemen, cutting off terrorist financing, combating extremism, and promoting maritime security.
“We stand shoulder-to-shoulder for a secure and stable Middle East in a peaceful and prosperous world,” he concluded.
Gen. Asseri also highlighted the efforts the Kingdom is taking in mobilizing the Muslim world against terrorism and extremism, describing the 41-nation Islamic Coalition created under Saudi leadership.
REGIONAL SECURITY
Saudi U.N.Delegation Hosts Yemen Coalition ExpertsSaudi Arabia’s Permanent Mission to the United Nations in New York convened a series of meetings from Feb.
27 to March 2 with military leaders, relief organizations and U.N.
organizations to promote the security and stability of Yemen.
A delegation of military leaders from the Coalition to Restore Legitimacy in Yemen, headed by Saudi Major General Misfer Al-Ghanem, met with John Ging, operations director of the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA).
Representatives of UNICEF, the UN Environment Program, the UN Population Fund, the UN Department of Safety and Security, the World Bank and the World Food Program (WFP) were also in attendance at the meetings.
HUMANITARIAN AID
Saudi Arabia Leads Efforts to Support Yemen’s Health Sector
The Riyadh-based King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSRelief) is leading international efforts to rehabilitate the health sector in Yemen and provide life-saving care to injured and ill Yemenis.
The health system in the country has been nearly destroyed since the Houthi militias and forces loyal to former president Ali Abdullah Saleh usurped power in 2014.
KSRelief – established in May 2015 – has carried out more than 50 projects in health, nutrition, water and environmental sanitation in Yemen, valued at more than $208 million and benefiting 24.5 million Yemeni citizens.
The organization also treats the injured in Yemen by providing medical services and treatment devices.
KSRelief has partnered with the UN International Children’s Emergency Fund (UNICEF) to train 3,153 health workers to manage acute malnutrition and opened 854 centers for a therapeutic nutrition program.
KSRelief mobile health teams have treated over a quarter of a million children suffering from acute malnutrition in Yemen and has provided immunization and postnatal care to over 1.2 million children through the country.
In collaboration with the World Health Organization (WHO), KSRelief has also provided basic services, including diabetes and cancer treatment, and supported the operation of emergency rooms by providing hundreds of thousands of galls of fuel to 88 hospitals and 19 health facilities throughout the country.
WOMEN’S HISTORY MONTH
Saudi Women Are Redefining Their RolesOn March 8, a group of Saudi women marked International Women’s Day by unveiling a campaign to highlight the changing role and economic potential of women in the Kingdom.
Saudi Arabia is in the midst of a great transformation.
Government reforms are diversifying the economy, revitalizing the cultural scene and modernizing the educational system.
Saudi women are in the vanguard of this transformation.
“It is the right time…we are more aware of the importance of women’s participation in jobs, in different sectors,” Princess Lamia bin Majed Al Saud, secretary general of Alwaleed Philanthropies, told Thomson Reuters Foundation.
Alwaleed Philanthropies is a charity that supports programs and policies to empower women.
She added, “Women have achieved a lot in our country and we are very proud of this, achievements the outside community does not know about.
Like any other country we have obstacles, we acknowledge that…but we are doing our best to make it better.”

title: Saudi Arabia in Focus
content:
This issue highlights Saudi-US relations, counterterrorism, regional security and Vision 2030. Read more about the Kingdom’s optimism in working with the Trump administration, Crown Prince Mohammad being honored by the U.S. for his counterterrorism efforts, Saudi Arabia as a “pillar of stability” in the region, and the World Economic Forum panel on Vision 2030. Saudi Arabia
FEBRUARY 2017
INSIDE THIS ISSUE
King Salman Receives Phone Call from U.S.Secretary of State
Foreign Minister Underscores Strength of Saudi-U.S.Partnership 
Saudi Crown Prince Honored by U.S. for Counterterrorism Efforts   
Security Forces Dismantle Four Daesh Cells   
Saudi Security Reports on Successful 2016 Counterterrorism Efforts
Safeguarding Civilians in Yemen Remains Foremost Priority
UNSG: Saudi a ‘Pillar of Stability’ in RegionWorld Economic Forum Panel on Vision 2030
NOTABLE QUOTE
“We are surrounded by areas of conflict, and we were the first [nation] affected by ter-rorism from various sources, but we are equipped to combat terror in any place and under any circumstances.”Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior Feb.10, 2017
SAUDI-U.S. RELATIONS
King Salman Receives Phone Call from U.S. Secretary of State 
On Feb.10, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received a phone call from U.S. Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.
King Salman congratulated the secretary on his recent appointment, and Sec.
Tillerson commended the leadership of Saudi Arabia in working for stability and peace both in the region and around the world, stressing that it is important for the United States and Saudi Arabia to work together.
Toward this end, it is critical to strengthen the military relationship, economic ties and cooperation in the fight against terrorism.
Foreign Minister Underscores Strength of Saudi-U.S. Partnership 
Speaking at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said that the U.S.-Saudi partnership is certain to strengthen in the future.
COUNTERTERRORISM
Saudi Crown Prince Honored by U.S. for Counterterrorism Efforts 
Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Inte-rior, was honored with the Central Intelligence Agency’s George Tenet Medal in recognition of his relentless counterterrorism efforts and commitment to international peace.
SAUDI-U.S. RELATIONS
Foreign Minister Underscores Strength of Saudi-U.S. Partnership
Speaking at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said that the U.S.-Saudi partnership is certain to strengthen in the future.
“The U.S. and Saudi Arabia have had a historic strategic partnership, if not alliance, since World War II; that relationship continues,” said Minister Al-Jubeir, adding that the two countries have had extraordinarily strong people-to-people relations, which are also expected to continue in the years to come.
“Our interests are aligned when it comes to the issues of the region,” he stressed.
At a separate press conference in Riyadh on Jan.24, Minister Al-Jubeir reiterated the importance of maintaining multi-faceted Saudi-U.S. cooperation, and stated that Saudi Arabia looks forward to working closely with the new U.S. administration led by President Donald Trump.
“We are very, very optimistic about the Trump administration.
And on working closely with it to deal with the many challenges, not only in our region, but in the world,” he said.
In an address to the Munich Security Conference on Feb.19, Minister Al-Jubeir said Saudi Arabia was optimistic about working with the U.S. administration.
“We expect to see America engaged in the world.
And we expect to see a realistic American foreign policy, and we look forward to working with this administration very, very closely,” he stated.
COUNTERTERRORISM
Saudi Crown Prince Honored by U.S. for Counterterrorism Efforts
Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, was honored with the Central Intelligence Agency’s George Tenet Medal in recognition of his relentless counterterrorism efforts and commitment to international peace.
The medal was presented to Prince Mohammad by CIA Director Mike Pompeo at a reception following their meeting in Riyadh on Feb.10.
Prince Mohammad stressed that the medal reflect-ed the commitment, hard work and policies of the leaders of Saudi Arabia, led by King Salman and the Saudi security forces, and cooperation by the entire Saudi community.
The Crown Prince emphasized that the fight against terrorism is a shared international obligation requiring global cooperation efforts at all levels, including “in security, intellectually, in the media and militarily.” He also emphasized that terrorism “has no identity and no religion,” noting that groups that exploit groups as a pretext for violence and extremism have no legitimate relationship with the religion to which they seek to associate themselves.
In response to a question on Saudi-U.S. relations, Prince Mohammad said that attempts to drive a wedge between the two countries will fail.
“Our relationship with the United States is historical and will continue to succeed,” he said.
Security Forces Dismantle Four Daesh Cells
In a successful counterinsurgency operation, the Ministry of Interior announced on Feb.16 that four Daesh (ISIS) cells were dismantled and 18 terrorists were arrested.
According to Ministry Spokesperson Maj.Gen.Mansour Al-Turki, 15 of those arrested were Saudi nationals, two were from Yemen and one was from Sudan.
All arrested were involved with varying aspects of Daesh operations, including the provision of financial and logistical support, offering shelter, and assisting with recruitment, training and the development of explosive devices for known operatives who have launched attacks in Saudi Arabia.
Gen.Al-Turki stated that the operation underscored Saudi Arabia’s forceful and decisive counterterrorism efforts and the ministry’s commitment to bringing to justice any individual involved in extremist activity.
Saudi Security Reports on Successful 2016 Counterterrorism Efforts
In January, Saudi security officials released data from 2016 on its efforts to combat terrorism in the country.
Saudi forces conducted eight operations that “foiled serious terrorist attacks.” The outcomes of the operations included the protection of one million Muslims at the Prophet’s Mosque, 60,000 spectators at the al-Jawhara Stadium in Jeddah and 400 civilians at al-Seef Restaurant and Café in Tarot.
The year also witnessed 190 arrests of Daesh-affiliated terrorists and the tactical prevention of a total of seven suicide attacks targeting mosques in the Eastern province.
In 2016, Saudi Arabia faced 34 domestic attacks from a number of extremist groups.
REGIONAL SECURITY
Safeguarding Civilians in Yemen Remains Foremost Priority
The Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen issued a statement on Feb.13 restating its commitment to protecting civilians in Yemen and underscoring steps the alliance has taken to avoid casualties in the war zone.
Among the steps taken, the Coalition has launched an independent assessment team, fostered active partnerships with relief organizations and focused on safeguarding the country’s urban centers to protect civilians and uphold international law.
The Joint Incidents Assessment Team (JIAT), established by the Coalition, is made up of military members, weapons experts and legal specialists.
The group, which operates independently, reviews reports of Coalition activities that have led to civilian casualties and offers recommendations to improve operations to avoid future incidents.
Additionally, the Coalition has adopted the policy to issue repeated warning to Houthi militias and Ali Abdullah Saleh forces to evacuate cities prior to a Coalition air strike.
The Coalition has also heightened its emphasis on safeguarding critical infrastructure and, as a result, civilian lives in Yemen’s urban centers.
The Coalition takes a collaborative approach by actively working alongside United Nations agencies and the International Committee of the Red Cross to minimize the possibility of harm to civilians, medical personnel, journalists and relief organizations.
UNSG: Saudi a ‘Pillar of Stability’ in RegionUnited Nations Secretary General Antonio Guterres said that Saudi Arabia was a “pillar of stability in the region” at a press conference in Riyadh, where he spoke alongside Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir.
“Saudi’s stability is a model for the pursuit of growth and change,” Guterres said, also stressing that “cooperation between the United Nations and Saudi Arabia is key in the fight against terrorism.” Minister Al-Jubeir and Secretary General Guterres reported that they had critical and constructive dis-cussions on possible political solutions for Yemen and Libya.
Guterres expressed appreciation for the hospi-tality he received in Saudi Arabia and the substantive dialogue that resulted from the meetings.
VISION 2030
World Economic Forum Panel on Vision 2030
Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030 plan commanded the attention of the World Economic Forum in January during a panel on the ongoing reforms and transformation of the country’s economy and society.
The session was hosted by Managing Director of the World Economic Forum Philipp Rosler and featured Saudi Arabia’s ministers of energy, finance and commerce along with the BlackRock CEO Laurence D.Fink and Dow Chemical’s CEO, Andrew N.Liveris.
Saudi Minister of Energy, Industry and Mineral Resources Khalid Al-Falih characterized investment and privatization as key to the vision in moving the country forward.
“Two key parts toward that are our young population, who are ready to turn into a workforce to be Mr.reckoned with, along with encouraging our women and investing in them as they increasingly join the workforce,” he said.
Finance Minister Al-Jadaan said Saudi Arabia is committed to becoming more accountable and transparent as it moves to diversify and privatize many of its sectors, stating that there are “orders from our leadership to hold each ministry to its goals and cutting down on bureaucracy.” 
Mr.Liveris drew on his 15 years of visiting the country.
“I can confirm that Saudi Arabia has full transparency, as much as compared with our experience in the United States.
This is especially apparent when we have multi-billion dollar dealings with the country,” he stated.

title: Saudi Arabia in Focus
content:
This issue highlights several topics on regional security, counterterrorism, Vision 2030, and humanitarian aid. Read more about King Salman’s commitment to regional security and economic development, the release of the 2017 budget and the projection of a major deficit reduction, and the kick-off of a relief campaign for the Syrian people. cholars Meet to Exchange Ideas on Countering Violent Extremism

“Our domestic policy  is based on pillars of  maintaining security,stability and prosperity to achieve the development that meets the needs of the present and preserves the right of future generations.
Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz Dec.14, 2016
Regional Security  
King Salman: Nation Is Committed  to Regional Security and Economic  Development  In his annual address to the Consultative Council on  Dec.14,  reinforced that Saudi Arabia remains fully committed to vigorous action that ensures its security and the security of the region while moving forward with the country’s internal economic development and reforms.
King Salman emphasized that Saudi Arabia has traditionally played an active and effective role on the regional and global stage and plans to proceed with “firm steps” that maintain its active role around the world.
He presented an optimistic outlook for both the nation’s domestic and foreign policies.
Counterterrorism
Security Forces Kill  2 Daesh Terrorists 
Saudi security forces killed two  terrorists affiliated with Daesh (ISIS)  in a district north of Riyadh on Jan.7.
Scholars Meet to Exchange Ideas on Countering Violent Extremism 
King Khalid University organized the second International Conference on Media and Terrorism, Methods and Strategies in early December 2016.
”Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz.
Regional Security 
King Salman: Nation Is Committed to Regional Security and  Economic Development
In his annual address to the Consultative Council on  Dec.14, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz reinforced that Saudi Arabia remains fully committed to vigorous action that  ensures its security and the security of the region while moving forward with the country’s internal  economic development and reforms.
King Salman emphasized that Saudi Arabia has  traditionally played an active and effective role on the regional and global stage and plans to proceed with “firm steps” that maintain its active role around the world.
He presented an optimistic outlook for both the nation’s domestic and foreign policies.
As part of Saudi Arabia’s core foreign policies, King Salman vowed to continue cooperation with the international community to combat extremism that threatens its borders and the larger region.
Political solutions to international crises can safeguard values of tolerance and coexistence and pave the way for nations to ensure peace and development, he said.
King Salman stressed that the region’s stability directly impacts Saudi Arabia’s economic development.
“Our domestic policy is based on pillars of maintaining security, stability and prosperity to achieve the development that meets the needs of the present and preserves the right of future generations,” he said.
Counterterrorism
Security Forces Kill 2 Daesh Terrorists 
Saudi security forces killed two terrorists affiliated with Daesh (ISIS) in a district north of Riyadh on Jan.7.
“Wanted terrorist Taie bin Salem bin Yaslem  Al-Saeiri was killed in a shootout with security forces after trying to escape with his companion, Talal bin Samran Al-Sa’edi,” said Spokesman of the Interior Ministry Major General Mansour Al-Turki.
Linked to Daesh, Al-Saeiri had the expertise to make bombs and explosive belts; he was also wanted for involvement in a 2016 attack on an emergency-forces mosque in the Asir region of Saudi Arabia.
Scholars Meet to Exchange Ideas on Countering Violent Extremism  
King Khalid University organized the second  International Conference on Media and Terrorism, Methods and Strategies in early December 2016.
Dr.Faleh bin Ra’allah Al-Sulami, university rector opened the conference. He remarked on the important role that universities have to play in  developing media that engages with modern issues, including violent extremism, according to Arab News.
“It is time [that] these specialized departments prepare experts who are capable of bearing responsibility, especially since terrorism has become an international phenomenon with terrorists surfing many sites on the internet,” Al-Sulami said.
Al-Sulami emphasized that Saudi Arabia has been affected by the global phenomenon of terrorism, but under steadfast leadership and national unity has “become a role model for many countries around the world in standing up to terrorism.” 
The conference featured more 80 research papers on the subject and engaged with more than 100 participants from inside and outside Saudi Arabia.
Conference organizers received more than 1,200 applications to participate in the conference, reflecting the critical nature of the work.
“The world has, through the edia, become a global village, and universities should exert greater efforts to study the phenomenon and make that study an integral part of their duty in serving the community,” Al-Sulaimi asserted.
Vision 2030
2017 Budget Released, Major Deficit Reduction Projected  
The Saudi government released its budget for 2017, outlining an 8 percent increase from 2016 and  projected a 33 percent decrease in the country’s  national deficit.
“We have sought through this budget and its  programs to improve the efficiency of capital and  operational expenditures in the state; strengthen the situation of public finances[and] enhance their sustainability; give priority to developmental and service projects and programs that serve citizens directly; and contribute to activating the role of the private sector and increasing its contribution to the Gross Domestic Product (GDP),” said King Salman.
Saudi Arabia’s revenue is projected to reach $184.5 billion in the 2017 fiscal year; oil revenues are forecasted to increase by 46 percent.
Vision 2030, released in April 2016, seeks to further strengthen the Saudi economy.
The budget reflects the government’s commitment to the reforms and initiatives laid out in the plan.
“The Kingdom’s fiscal policy aims to strengthen the general finance framework and boost efficiency in government spending to reach a balanced budget.
This is in line with the Kingdom’s Vision 2030 and related programs,” the budget states.
Businesses Visas to be Granted Within 24 Hours  
A new mechanism has been put in place to encourage investment in Saudi Arabia: Foreign commercial businesses looking to set up shop in the country will be granted visas within 24 hours.
The application system for businesses was opened on Jan.1, 2017. 
Encouraging foreign investment in the Saudi Arabia is a key part of Vision 2030, and the effort to ease access to the Saudi market aims  to advance this goal.
The new visa measure was introduced as a result of directives issued by Saudi Arabia’s Council of Economic and Development Affairs, which is charged with improving the investment environment and generating increased business activity in the private sector.
The Saudi government is looking to increase foreign direct investment from 3.8 percent to the international level of 5.7 percent of the country’s GDP by 2030.
Humanitarian Aid 
King Salman Orders Relief Campaign for the Syrian People
On Dec.26, 2016, King Salman ordered the execution of a relief campaign to ease the suffering of the Syrian people who have been displaced, especially those evacuated from Aleppo and other conflict areas, who face a particularly difficult situation.
King Salman dedicated $27 million for the campaign.
The campaign will be coordinated by the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works (KSRELIEF), which will set up collection centers throughout the Kingdom to collect monetary and in-kind donations.
King Salman kicked off the campaign by donating $5.3 million, Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif donated $2.1 million, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman donated $2.1 million.

title: Counterterrorism Update
content:
On Oct. 20, Saudi Arabia designated as supporters of terrorism two individuals and one business over their links to Hezbollah.Those sanctioned are Lebanese nationals Muhammad Al-Mukhtar Falah Kallas and Hasan Jamal-al-Din, and the Iraq-and Lebanon-based Global Cleaners (aka: GLBAL CLEN S.A.R.L.).The individuals and business sanctioned were designated under the Saudi Law of Terrorism Crimes and Financing and Royal Decree A/44, which targets terrorists and their supporters that act for or on behalf of terrorist organizations. Under the law, any assets under Saudi jurisdiction are frozen, and Saudi citizens or residents are generally prohibited from engaging in any transactions with the individuals or entities. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia

NOVEMBER 2016
TOP NEWS
INSIDE THIS ISSUE
Saudi Arabia Sanctions Individuals and Company Linked to Hezbollah
Meetings Planned to Defeat ExtremismSaudi Authorities Foil Terrorist Attack 
Saudi Officials Arrest Five on Terrorism Charges  
Saudi Arabia to Provide Counterterrorism Training  to Malaysia\
NOTABLE QUOTE “Saudi Arabia experienced a six-fold increase in deaths [due to terrorism] compared to the prior year, with 48  attacks resulting in 107 deaths in 2015.
This is the highest level of terrorism Saudi Arabia has experi-enced since at least 2000.
There were more people killed from terrorism in 2015 in Saudi Arabia than in the previous 11 years combined.”
Global Terrorism Index 2016The Institute for Economics and Peace
The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Counterterrorism Update Saudi Arabia Sanctions Individuals and Company Linked  to HezbollahOn Oct.20, Saudi Arabia designated as supporters of terrorism two individuals and one business over their links to Hezbollah.
Saudi Arabia Sanctions Individuals and Company Linked to Hezbollah
On Oct.20, Saudi Arabia designated as supporters of  terrorism two individuals and one business over their links to Hezbollah.
Those sanctioned are Lebanese nationals Muhammad Al-Mukhtar Falah Kallas and Hasan Jamal-al-Din, and the Iraq-and Lebanon-based Global Cleaners (aka: GLBAL CLEN S.A.R.L.).
The individuals and business sanctioned were designated under the Saudi Law  of Terrorism Crimes and Financing and Royal Decree A/44, which targets terrorists and their supporters that act for or on behalf of terrorist organizations.
Under the law, any assets under Saudi jurisdiction are frozen, and Saudi citizens or residents are generally prohibited from engaging in any transactions with the individuals  or entities.
Meetings Planned to  Defeat Extremism
Saudi Authorities Foil Terrorist Attack
The King Abdulaziz Center for Nation-al Dialogue (KACND) is intensifying efforts to boost national cohesion through societal engagement to com-bat extremism and deviant ideology.
Saudi security officials at the Ministry of Interior announced on Oct.30 that they foiled a plot to bomb the football stadi-um at King Abdullah Sports City during a World Cup qualifying match between Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.
Those sanctioned are Lebanese nationals Muhammad Al-Mukhtar Falah Kallas and Hasan Jamal-al-Din, and the Iraq-and Lebanon-based Global Cleaners (aka: GLBAL CLEN S.A.R.L.).
The individuals and business sanctioned were desig-nated under the Saudi Law of Terrorism Crimes and Financing and Royal Decree A/44, which targets ter-rorists and their supporters that act for or on behalf of terrorist organizations.
Under the law, any assets under Saudi jurisdiction are frozen, and Saudi citizens or residents are generally prohibited from engaging  in any transactions with the individuals or entities.
The Kingdom will continue to label those linked to Hezbollah as terrorists and sanction the individuals or groups according to the Kingdom’s laws designed to combat terrorism.
Saudi Arabia has put in place one of the world’s strictest financial control systems in order to prevent funds from going to support terrorism.
Today, Saudi Arabia is one of the leading nations in combating terrorism and terrorist financing, and has been working closely with its allies on all fronts.
Meetings Planned to Defeat Extremism
The King Abdulaziz Center for National Dialogue (KACND) is intensifying efforts to boost national cohesion through societal engagement to combat extremism and deviant ideology.
On Oct.6, the center announced a new initiative, dubbed “Talahom,” which will promote meetings aimed at putting into practice the principles of co-ex-istence and national unity.
Secretary-General of KACND, Faisal Al-Muammar, stressed the importance of the initiative as the first step in multiple projects that will be run by the center in the coming year.
He added that these types of projects preserve and enhance the Kingdom’s security and strengthen national unity based on shared values.
“What prompted KACND to organize weekly dia-logues for the Talahom project is the importance  attached to the community participation at all levels to support security and intellectual efforts and  promote moderation,” said Al-Muammar.
Saudi Authorities Foil Terrorist Attack 
Saudi security officials at the Ministry of Interior  announced on Oct.30 that they foiled a plot to bomb the football stadium at King Abdullah Sports City during a World Cup qualifying match between Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.
Spokesman for the Ministry of Interior Major General Mansour Al-Turki said that the authorities were able to identify and prevent plans aimed at the Kingdom by monitoring of terrorist threats targeting security installations in the Kingdom and dealing with those linked to Daesh abroad.
General Mansour noted that law enforcement  agencies raided a terrorist cell made up of four peo-ple, based in Shaqraa.
The cell was planning activities  targeting security forces based on orders from a Daesh terrorist leader in Syria.
All four members of the cell were arrested.
They were identified as Ahmad bin Mohammed bin Homood Al-Maeeli, Abdullah bin Obaid bin Mihmas Al-Osaimi Al-Otaibi, Abdulaziz bin Faisal bin Jafeen Al-Daajani Al-Otaibi, and Mujahid bin Rashid bin Mohammed  Al-Rashid.
Al-Turki said that during investigations the individuals admitted a direct link between the cell and members of Daesh in Syria, and that they had been monitoring a number of security officers working in Tabuk, Riyadh and the Eastern Province, pending instructions from abroad to target them with violent acts.
Six other members also were arrested for their links with the cell members.
Additional investigations are underway to identify their exact connection with the cell and its activities.
Saudi Officials Arrest Five on Terrorism Charges
Police arrested five people — four foreigners and one Saudi — in September for links to terrorist groups and recent terrorism operations in Saudi Arabia.
The Kingdom is pursuing the men, the money, and the mindsets that support this global threat.
It has forged partnerships with multilateral organizations and foreign governments to protect national security and reinforce counterterrorism strategies.
Saudi Arabia to Provide Counterterrorism Training to Malaysia
The government of Malaysia is sending 10 senior se-curity specialists to Saudi Arabia to study, do research and exchange ideas at the Prince Mohammad bin Naif Center for Security Studies.
“We want to benefit from the experience of the King-dom in counseling and countering extremist ideology with moderation, argument and proof,” said the  Malaysian Ambassador to Saudi Arabia, Zainol  Rahim Zainuddin.
Hamidi said that he discussed with King Salman  solutions to protect Southeast Asian countries from the threat of terrorism carried out by Daesh (ISIS), which has recruited individuals from these countries.
In the discussion, an understanding was reached that Saudi Arabia and Malaysia would continue to exchange knowledge on experiences with combating terrorism and extremist ideology.
In addition, Hamidi praised the counseling practices applied by Saudi Arabia, pointing to the Prince  Mohammad bin Naif Center as a “unique model to counter extremist ideology with moderate ideology.”  3The Kingdom of Saudi ArabiaCounterterrorism Update ROYAL EMBASSY OF SAUDI ARABIAINFORMATION OFFICEWASHINGTON, D.C.

title: Saudi Vision 2030 Status Report
content:
ENERGY MINISTER AFFIRMS COMMITMENT TO VISION 2030“It is not unthinkable” that the price of oil could jump to $60 per barrel by the end of the year, said Saudi Minister of Energy, Industry, and Mineral Resources Khalid Al-Falih in recent remarks at the 23rd World Energy Congress in Istanbul. According to Al-Falih, the fluctuating price of oil has not affected the Kingdom’s preparations to implement the Vision 2030 plan, which will diversify the Saudi economy and invite investment across a broad array of sectors. INSIDE THIS ISSUE
Energy Minister Affirms Commitment to Vision 2030
